## Meeting Minutes

### Attendance

#### Voting Members

- Ms. Myrtice Johnson, President
- Mr. Daryl Morton, Vice President
- Mrs. Kristin Hanlon, Treasurer
- Dr. Henry Ficklin, Board Member
- Mr. Barney Hester, Board Member
- Dr. Sundra Woodford, Board Member
- Mr. James Freeman, Board Member
- Dr. Lisa Garrett-Boyd, Board Member

### I. Call to Order

The Committee Meeting of the Bibb County Board of Education was called to order at 4 p.m.

### II. Approve Agenda

A motion was made to amend the agenda by removing FSS-2 (Annual Renewal of the Continuation Pre-K Grant); adding FSS-11 (Multi-Year Lease Purchase Agreement with Lenovo Financial Services); and adding PS-6 (Central Office Recommendations).

- Motion made by: Mr. James Freeman
- Motion seconded by: Mr. Daryl Morton

_Voting results:_ Unanimously approved

### III. INVOCATION

The invocation was given by Dr. Henry Ficklin.

### IV. INSTRUCTIONAL SERVICES COMMITTEE

#### i. IS-1 School Consolidation Update and Recommendation (PRESENTATION)

#### ii. IS-2 Safety and Security Updates (PRESENTATION)

### V. FISCAL/SUPPORT SERVICES COMMITTEE

#### i. FSS-1 Monthly Financial Statement Review (PRESENTATION)

#### ii. FSS-3 Mathematics Instructional Resources Adoption Approval (ACTION)

The Bibb County Board of Education authorized the Superintendent to approve the Mathematics Instructional Resource adoption and purchase Mathematics textbooks and instructional materials in an amount not to exceed $5,515,711.09.

- Motion made by: Dr. Henry Ficklin
- Motion seconded by: Dr. Lisa Garrett-Boyd

_Voting results:_ Unanimously approved

#### iii. FSS-4 Architectural Services for the Renovations of Innovation & Technology Academy Project Phase 2 (Formerly Butler Collision Center) (ACTION)

The Bibb County Board of Education authorized the Superintendent to approve an amended contract with WM2A Architects to provide architectural services for Phase 2 renovations of the Innovation & Technology Academy (Formerly Butler Collision Center).

- Motion made by: Mr. James Freeman
- Motion seconded by: Dr. Henry Ficklin

_Voting results:_ Unanimously approved

#### iv. FSS-5 Construction Management At-Risk Services for the Renovations of Innovation & Technology Academy Project Phase 2 (Formerly Butler Collision Center) (ACTION)

The Bibb County Board of Education authorized the Superintendent to approve an amended contract with Warren Associates, Inc. to provide Construction Manager At-Risk Services for renovations to Innovation & Technology Academy (Formerly Butler Collision Center) Phase 2.

- Motion made by: Dr. Sundra Woodford
- Motion seconded by: Mr. James Freeman

_Voting results:_ Unanimously approved

#### v. FSS-6 RFP 25-023 Architectural Services for Northwoods Academy Gymnasium (ACTION)

The Bibb County Board of Education authorized the Superintendent to enter into contract with SP Design Group to provide architectural services for the construction of a Gymnasium at Northwoods Academy.

- Motion made by: Dr. Lisa Garrett-Boyd
- Motion seconded by: Mr. Daryl Morton

_Voting results:_ Unanimously approved

#### vi. FSS-7 School Bus Purchase (ACTION)

The Bibb County Board of Education authorized the Superintendent to issue a purchase order in an amount not to exceed $1,499,000.00 to purchase ten (10) school buses from Yancey Bus Sales utilizing Georgia Statewide Contract 99999-SPD-SWL20200630-001.

- Motion made by: Mr. Daryl Morton
- Motion seconded by: Mr. James Freeman

_Voting results:_ Unanimously approved

#### vii. FSS-8 Student Chromebooks FY26 (ACTION)

The Bibb County Board of Education entertained a motion to authorize the purchase order to SureLock Technology for student Chromebooks in an amount of $2,919,243.58 for the first of four payments (2025, 2026, 2027, 2028).

Upon a request for clarification from Mr. Daryl Morton and advice of legal counsel, Mrs. Sherry Culves, the initial motion was amended as follows:

The Bibb County Board of Education authorizes the purchase order to SureLock Technology for student Chromebooks in an amount of $2,919,243.58 for the first of four payments (2025, 2026, 2027, 2028) contingent upon the passage of FSS-11, Multi-Year Lease Purchase Agreement with Lenovo Financial Services.

Initial Motion made by: Dr. Lisa Garrett-Boyd

Initial Motion seconded by: Ms.  Myrtice Johnson

Voting: Unanimously Approved

Amended Motion made by: Mr. James Freeman

Voting: Unanimously Approved

#### viii. FSS-9 Lightspeed Renewal – FY26 (ACTION)

The Bibb County Board of Education authorized the purchase order to CDW for Lightspeed products in an amount not to exceed $189,530.00.

- Motion made by: Dr. Henry Ficklin
- Motion seconded by: Mr. Daryl Morton

_Voting results:_ Unanimously approved

#### ix. FSS-10 Microsoft License Renewal FY26 (ACTION)

The Bibb County Board of Education authorized the purchase order to Dell for the District’s FY26 licensing of Microsoft EDU Office 365 Pro Plus productsin the amount not to exceed $432,810.90.

- Motion made by: Mr. James Freeman
- Motion seconded by: Dr. Lisa Garrett-Boyd

_Voting results:_ Unanimously approved

#### x. FSS-11 Multi-Year Lease Purchase Agreement with Lenovo Financial Services (ACTION)

The Bibb County Board of Education adopted a Resolution authorizing the District to enter into this Lease Agreement.

- Motion made by: Mr. Daryl Morton
- Motion seconded by: Mr. James Freeman

_Voting results:_ Unanimously approved

### VI. POLICIES AND RULES COMMITTEE

#### i. PR-1 Policy GARHA, Employee Sick Leave Bank (ACTION - Second Reading)

The Bibb County Board of Education approved revisions to Policy GARHA, Employee Sick Leave Bank.

- Motion made by: Mrs. Kristin Hanlon
- Motion seconded by: Mr. Barney Hester

_Voting results:_ Unanimously approved

#### ii. PR-2 Policy JBCCA, Student Assignment to Schools (ACTION - Second Reading)

The Bibb County Board of Education approved a motion to postpone the vote on Policy JBCCA, Student Assignment to Schools, until further review and re-submission during next month's Committee meeting.

- Motion made by: Mr. Daryl Morton
- Motion seconded by: Mr. James Freeman

_Voting results:_ Unanimously approved

#### iii. PR-3 Policy AFC, Emergency Closings (ACTION - Second Reading)

The Bibb County Board of Education acknowledged that there were no revisions to Policy AFC, Emergency Closings.

- Motion made by: Dr. Henry Ficklin
- Motion seconded by: Mr. James Freeman

_Voting results:_ Unanimously approved

#### iv. PR-4 Policy DIA, Accounting System (INFORMATION - First Reading)

#### v. PR-5 Policy EDDA, Special Use of School Buses (INFORMATION - First Reading)

#### vi. PR-6 Policy IFCD, School Volunteers (INFORMATION - First Reading)

The Board voted to enter into Executive Session at 6:03 p.m.

Motion made by: Dr. Sundra Woodford

Motion seconded by: Mr. Daryl Morton

Voting: Unanimously Approved

The Board voted to return from Executive Session at 6:38 p.m.

Motion made by:  Dr. Sundra Woodford

Motion seconded by: Dr. Henry Ficklin

Voting: Unanimously Approved

Mr. James Freeman made a motion to amend the Committee meeting agenda by removing PS-6 and placing it on the Board meeting agenda under New Business.

Motion made by: Dr. Sundra Woodford

Motion seconded by: Mrs. Kristin Hanlon

Voting: Unanimously Approved

#### ii. PS-1 Certified Personnel Report (ACTION)

The Board voted to approve PS-1.

- Motion made by: Dr. Sundra Woodford
- Motion seconded by: Mr. Daryl Morton

_Voting results:_ Unanimously approved

#### iii. PS-2 Classified Personnel Report (ACTION)

The Board voted to approve PS-2.

- Motion made by: Dr. Lisa Garrett-Boyd
- Motion seconded by: Dr. Henry Ficklin

_Voting results:_ Unanimously approved

#### iv. PS-3 Classified Personnel Report (INFORMATION)

The Board voted to approve PS-5.

- Motion made by: Dr. Henry Ficklin
- Motion seconded by: Dr. Sundra Woodford

_Voting results:_ Unanimously approved

#### vi. PS-6 Central Office Recommendation (ACTION)
